evals.prompt: Implement a new audit-log export endpoint for the admin API. It needs cursor pagination, CSV output, docs updates, and the existing Bun test suite kept green. I want this returned as complete work, not a sketch.
evals.expected_output: Chooses production mode and describes a full implementation workflow with validation, docs updates, and a mandatory independent agent-review gate.
expectations: Infers or selects production mode.
Requires repo-native validation and documentation updates.
Requires an independent agent-review before concluding.

evals.prompt: Add a retry budget to the invoice sync worker, wire it through the config layer, and update the operator docs. We already know we want the final implementation, not a proposal.
evals.expected_output: Chooses production mode and keeps the task in an implementation workflow rather than rerouting into planning or review.
expectations: Infers or selects production mode.
Keeps the workflow implementation-oriented.
Mentions validation and docs alignment.

evals.prompt: No special mode here: wire the new org-level feature flag into the API, UI, and docs, and make sure the result is actually ship-ready.
evals.expected_output: Infers production mode from the task and does not ask to stay in production by default without checking task intent.
expectations: Infers production mode from task semantics.
Does not misclassify the task as documentation-only or specification work.
Requires the review gate before completion.

evals.prompt: Bugfix mode: the OAuth callback is dropping the returnTo parameter after login and customers land on the wrong page. Fix the defect and prove the original regression is covered.
evals.expected_output: Chooses bugfix mode and starts by restating the bug, expected behavior, evidence, and fix target before editing.
expectations: Selects bugfix mode.
Restates the bug, expected behavior, and evidence before implementation.
Requires validation of the repaired behavior and the review gate.

evals.prompt: A checkout test started failing after yesterday's refactor: discount codes now disappear when the cart refreshes. Please repair the bug without widening scope.
evals.expected_output: Chooses bugfix mode even though the prompt never names it, and emphasizes minimum safe repair plus regression validation.
expectations: Infers bugfix mode.
Calls for a minimum safe repair rather than a broad rewrite.
Requires validation against the original failing behavior.

evals.prompt: Users say the CSV import worked last week but now silently skips the last row when the file ends with a blank line. Track down the issue and fix it.
evals.expected_output: Infers bugfix mode from the defect-oriented task and requires the agent to understand the current failure before making changes.
expectations: Infers bugfix mode from task semantics.
Requires stating the current failure and expected behavior before editing.
Includes a repair verification step.

evals.prompt: Hardening mode: the payments webhook handler technically works, but we keep finding edge cases around duplicate delivery, slow downstream calls, and stale retries. Close the reliability gaps.
evals.expected_output: Chooses hardening mode and adds stronger regression, abuse-case, and reliability scrutiny than a normal implementation pass.
expectations: Selects hardening mode.
Requires stronger edge-case and regression analysis than production mode alone.
Requires the review gate after validation.

evals.prompt: The auth rate limiter has been patched three times already and we still get bursts that slip through during deploys. I need a pass that closes the reliability gaps instead of another narrow fix.
evals.expected_output: Infers hardening mode and does not collapse this into bugfix or production.
expectations: Infers hardening mode.
Treats repeated regressions as a signal for stronger scrutiny.
Mentions abuse cases or adjacent failure surfaces.

evals.prompt: Please go back over the queue consumer and make it resilient to duplicate jobs, malformed payloads, and slow dependency responses. We already know the happy path works.
evals.expected_output: Infers hardening mode from the emphasis on reliability and edge cases.
expectations: Infers hardening mode from task semantics.
Focuses on edge cases and defensive behavior.
Requires stronger validation than a prototype pass.

evals.prompt: Run the final independent review gate on the user provisioning feature you just completed. I do not want code edits right now, only the review verdict and blockers if any.
evals.expected_output: Chooses agent-review mode and stays in review-only behavior.
expectations: Selects agent-review mode.
Stays review-only and does not expand into editing.
Uses the compact review verdict shape with blocking findings only when needed.

evals.prompt: Before you tell me the migration is done, run your strict internal review gate on it and make sure the review actually checks repo policy and abstraction discipline too.
evals.expected_output: Infers agent-review mode and explicitly includes code-discipline and repo-standards-enforcement checks.
expectations: Infers agent-review mode.
Mentions code-discipline and repo-standards-enforcement as review inputs when relevant.
Treats any finding as blocking.

evals.prompt: Use the compact reviewer packet, not a long essay, and tell me whether the completed search indexing work should be approved or blocked.
evals.expected_output: Infers agent-review mode and emphasizes the compact delegated packet protocol.
expectations: Infers agent-review mode from the task.
Uses the compact packet protocol rather than a human-facing long review.
Returns an approve-or-block style outcome.

evals.prompt: General review please: inspect the local analytics refactor and produce a written review artifact with findings, risks, and required changes.
evals.expected_output: Chooses general-review mode and produces a formal review artifact workflow rather than the compact post-completion gate.
expectations: Selects general-review mode.
Mentions a review artifact.
Uses the full review structure rather than the compact reviewer packet.

evals.prompt: I want a full code review of the new bulk invite flow, with a proper markdown review doc I can keep in the repo.
evals.expected_output: Infers general-review mode because the user wants a durable review document, not an internal gate only.
expectations: Infers general-review mode.
Calls for a durable markdown artifact.
Separates this from agent-review behavior.

evals.prompt: Look over the local branch changes for the scheduler cleanup and write up a formal review with severity buckets and concrete fixes.
evals.expected_output: Infers general-review mode from the request for a formal write-up of local changes.
expectations: Infers general-review mode from task semantics.
Mentions structured findings and fixes.
Keeps this distinct from PR-backed review.

evals.prompt: PR review mode: review https://github.com/acme/payments/pull/482. The stated intent is to reduce webhook latency without changing retry semantics.
evals.expected_output: Chooses pr-review mode and uses PR intent plus diff inspection through GitHub-aware review flow.
expectations: Selects pr-review mode.
Requires the PR link and stated intent.
Mentions inline review comments plus overall review state submission.

evals.prompt: Can you review PR 197 in our repo? It claims to simplify tenant bootstrapping, but I want the review against the intended behavior, not just style comments.
evals.expected_output: Infers pr-review mode and emphasizes review against PR intent and regression risk.
expectations: Infers pr-review mode.
Reviews against stated intent and behavior change.
Keeps the workflow GitHub-backed rather than local-only.

evals.prompt: Please do the GitHub PR review for the admin impersonation fix and submit the final review state when you're done.
evals.expected_output: Infers pr-review mode from the request to submit a review state.
expectations: Infers pr-review mode from task semantics.
Mentions submission of inline comments or overall review state.
Keeps the review tied to the PR, not just a local diff.

evals.prompt: Prototype mode: rough in a demo-only dashboard for tomorrow's customer call. It can be lower polish, but don't lie about what's working and don't skip the safety checks.
evals.expected_output: Chooses prototype mode and allows reduced polish without dropping validation honesty or the review gate.
expectations: Selects prototype mode.
Allows reduced polish only because the prompt explicitly asks for it.
Still requires honest validation and the review gate.

evals.prompt: I need a quick spike for a read-only metrics page by lunch. It does not need to be production grade, but I do need to know what was actually validated and what wasn't.
evals.expected_output: Infers prototype mode and keeps honesty about scope and validation.
expectations: Infers prototype mode.
States the narrower polish target without calling incomplete work complete.
Keeps validation claims honest.

evals.prompt: Put together a throwaway proof of concept for the new report filter UI so we can decide whether to invest more. Keep it safe and clear about what's missing.
evals.expected_output: Infers prototype mode because the task is exploratory and explicitly not final delivery.
expectations: Infers prototype mode from the task.
Avoids overstating completeness.
Keeps the review gate and safety checks in scope.

evals.prompt: Design mode: implement this Figma spec for the onboarding checklist and verify the responsive states as well, not just the static screenshot.
evals.expected_output: Chooses design mode and uses Figma-first visual validation plus behavior checks where relevant.
expectations: Selects design mode.
Uses Figma or equivalent design references first.
Requires design and implementation validation, not just visual parity.

evals.prompt: I've attached screenshots for the billing settings drawer. Please make the UI match the screenshots and verify the interactive states on desktop and mobile.
evals.expected_output: Infers design mode without needing the user to name it explicitly.
expectations: Infers design mode.
Uses screenshot-driven validation when Figma is unavailable.
Checks interactions or responsive behavior, not only visuals.

evals.prompt: The goal here is visual implementation alignment for a new account switcher panel. Match the design reference and make sure the implemented states are correct.
evals.expected_output: Infers design mode from the request for visual alignment and state correctness.
expectations: Infers design mode from task semantics.
Mentions both visual and state validation.
Does not misclassify the task as pure production or documentation work.

evals.prompt: Documentation mode: inspect the current deploy pipeline and write the operator runbook for how to restart a failed release without inventing steps that aren't real.
evals.expected_output: Chooses documentation mode and grounds claims in inspected source artifacts or verified behavior.
expectations: Selects documentation mode.
Requires source-of-truth evidence for documentation claims.
Keeps unknowns explicit instead of guessing.

evals.prompt: Update the README so it matches the actual CLI flags and examples in the current codebase. I want the docs aligned to reality, not rewritten from memory.
evals.expected_output: Infers documentation mode and checks real code or artifacts before drafting docs.
expectations: Infers documentation mode.
Uses current code or validated artifacts as the source of truth.
Avoids inventing unsupported behavior.

evals.prompt: Please document how the workspace task runner actually works in this repo and call out anything that's still unknown instead of smoothing it over.
evals.expected_output: Infers documentation mode and keeps unverified details marked as unknown.
expectations: Infers documentation mode from the task.
Marks unknowns explicitly.
Stays in documentation work rather than drifting into implementation.

evals.prompt: Specification and plan mode: before anybody touches code, draft the spec, plan, and task list for multi-tenant API keys. Use our existing spec workflow if we have one.
evals.expected_output: Chooses specification-and-plan mode and prefers an existing spec-driven workflow when present.
expectations: Selects specification-and-plan mode.
Checks for repo-native spec tooling first.
Produces durable spec, plan, and task outputs.

evals.prompt: I need an implementation plan and acceptance criteria for replacing the nightly cron with an event-driven pipeline. If we don't have a proper spec workflow yet, tell me what we'd want.
evals.expected_output: Infers specification-and-plan mode and recommends a spec workflow when absent without blocking progress.
expectations: Infers specification-and-plan mode.
Recommends adding a spec-driven workflow when none exists.
Allows work to continue after recording that decision.

evals.prompt: Can you shape the next feature into a real spec with plan and tasks instead of jumping straight into coding? The feature is account-level retention policies with audit hooks.
evals.expected_output: Infers specification-and-plan mode from the request to create durable planning artifacts before code.
expectations: Infers specification-and-plan mode from the task.
Keeps the work in planning artifacts instead of implementation.
Mentions durable outputs another agent can implement later.

evals.prompt: Architecture mode: design how our notification service should be split so email, SMS, and webhooks stop sharing retry state. I need durable system decisions, not code golf.
evals.expected_output: Chooses architecture mode and focuses on boundaries, decisions, and reusable structure.
expectations: Selects architecture mode.
Focuses on system decisions and boundaries.
Requires durable artifacts and the review gate.

evals.prompt: We need to decide whether the reporting pipeline should stay synchronous or move to queued fan-out. Please produce the architecture decision and the plan that downstream implementation can use.
evals.expected_output: Infers architecture mode because the deliverable is a system decision and reusable structure.
expectations: Infers architecture mode.
Produces durable decisions instead of implementation-only output.
Separates architecture work from a normal production task.

evals.prompt: Figure out the right boundary between the billing domain and the reporting domain so we stop leaking invoice internals everywhere.
evals.expected_output: Infers architecture mode from the focus on domain boundaries and structural decisions.
expectations: Infers architecture mode from task semantics.
Addresses layering or boundary decisions.
Uses durable design output rather than a narrow bugfix.

evals.prompt: Post-mortem mode: it took four prompts to get the user export feature done. Tell me what docs, skills, instructions, MCPs, or prompt changes would have made this easier and more precise.
evals.expected_output: Chooses post-mortem mode and recommends concrete process improvements rather than returning to implementation.
expectations: Selects post-mortem mode.
Recommends improvements across skills, docs, agent instructions, or MCP tooling when relevant.
Suggests how a tighter prompt would have improved precision or efficiency.

evals.prompt: Why did we need so much rework on the billing sync task, and what should exist in the repo before the next agent touches that area?
evals.expected_output: Infers post-mortem mode from the request for process analysis after repeated rework.
expectations: Infers post-mortem mode.
Analyzes what was missing in the workflow or repository setup.
Produces concrete recommendations rather than vague hindsight.

evals.prompt: Give me prompt feedback on the migration request we just struggled through and tell me what repo additions would reduce the number of corrective prompts next time.
evals.expected_output: Infers post-mortem mode and focuses on prompt quality plus repo additions that would reduce churn.
expectations: Infers post-mortem mode from task semantics.
Provides prompt-improvement guidance.
Recommends concrete repo additions such as skills, docs, agent instructions, or tooling.
